---
title: "expdpy"
subtitle: "Explore your panel data interactively"
---

**expdpy** is a Python port of the [ExPanDaR](https://github.com/trr266/ExPanDaR) R package
(Joachim Gassen, TRR 266). It gives you a set of composable analytical functions and an
interactive web app for exploratory analysis of panel and cross-sectional data, built on the
modern Python stack:

- **[Plotly](https://plotly.com/python/)** — interactive figures
- **[pyfixest](https://github.com/py-econometrics/pyfixest)** — fixed-effects / clustered regressions
- **[Great Tables](https://posit-dev.github.io/great-tables/)** — publication-quality tables
- **[Shiny for Python](https://shiny.posit.co/py/)** — the no-code `ExPanD` app

## Installation

```bash
pip install expdpy          # core analytical functions
pip install "expdpy[app]"   # + the interactive ExPanD app
```

## At a glance

```python
import expdpy as ex
from expdpy.data import load_russell_3000

df = load_russell_3000()
ex.prepare_descriptive_table(df[["sales", "ni_sales", "nioa"]]).gt
```

Head to the [Quickstart](quickstart.qmd) to see every function in action, or the
[ExPanD tutorial](tutorials/using-expand.qmd) to launch the interactive app.

## Why a port?

ExPanDaR is a wonderful tool for assessing the robustness of empirical evidence without
sharing the underlying data. `expdpy` brings the same workflow to Python users and
integrates with the `pandas` / `pyfixest` ecosystem. See
[Parity with ExPanDaR](explanation/parity.qmd) for how numerical fidelity is verified.
